summary:
Yes, many colorectal cancers result from missed adenomas because a significant proportion of adenomas go undetected during routine colonoscopies, especially those smaller than 5mm. These undiagnosed precursor polyps can progress into malignancy over time.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{yes}}$

The summary is correct but includes an extra sentence beyond what was requested. Please revise strictly within two sentences only.

Only include essential facts directly tied to the core medical point about missing adenoma leading to CRC development.

Many colorectal cancers originate from missed adenomas, particularly small ones less than 5 mm, which frequently escape detection during colonoscopy despite being precancerous. Undetected adenomatous polyps have been shown to progress to carcinoma over time.
